query: CRT television static screen flicker old TV monitor
message: anti-aesthetic / clarity_and_focus / analog_degradation: CRT television static and old TV monitors

response.objective_caption: A white CRT monitor with a black screen sits on a shelf against a dark background. The monitor has a Compaq logo and a red label on the lower bezel, with several small control buttons and a larger circular button centered below the screen. Blue fluorescent light reflections are visible on the glossy screen surface, and faint room reflections including what appears to be a person can be seen in the dark glass. A small red "COMPAQ" badge is visible on the lower left corner of the frame.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A white CRT monitor with a black screen sits on a shelf against a dark background, its screen so poorly reflective and lacking in detail that only dim, muddy silhouettes and blue light streaks appear in the glass. The monitor has a Compaq logo and a red label on the lower bezel, with several small control buttons and a larger circular button centered below the screen. A small red "COMPAQ" badge is visible on the lower left corner of the frame. The flat, directionless lighting collapses the scene into a dull, dimensionless surface with no shadow depth or spatial definition, giving the entire image a suffocating, heavy atmosphere.
